frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Frankly, young men should insist on a prenup now. Aajkal marriage has become risky for men also—one 498A complaint or divorce, and even without proof the man and his family suffer for years. We keep calling dowry evil, rightly so, but huge alimony in many cases is nothing but reverse dowry in a legal form.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financial threats in marriage and casts legal protections such as 498A and alimony as tools women exploit against men. It encodes the surface manifestations of 'reverse dowry,' 'protection from women' via prenups, and generalized claims about misuse of laws.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
